_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description:
You can:
- put in place processes that identify and document risks across the organisation and embed them into wider governance processes
- make plans to deal with issues caused by different types of risks
- use risk assessments to monitor data quality and compliance and whether risks are decreasing
Examples of leadership using this skill:
- getting owners of data assets to take responsibility for risks
- helping people use risk assessment results to make decisions instead of relying on opinion
- creating an environment where risk standards, policies and processes are valued
- encouraging the organisation to use and share data whilst ensuring data is appropriately protected
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting and reviewing Data Protection Impact Assessments, identifying processing activities that present high risk to individuals, and recommending proportionate technical and organizational measures to mitigate identified risks.
comments: DDaT skill equal WIDAI SP domain. SP-S-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-K-009 (SP, cosine: 0.634), RC-S-004 (RC, cosine: 0.630).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.0/10 (raw 0.600). Shared vocabulary: [assessments, data, risk, risks, skill]. DDaT-only terms [across, appropriately, assessment, assets, caused, compliance] vs WIDAI-only terms [activities, conducting, high, identified, identifying, impact] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.842 (P=0.822, R=0.862). R exceeds P by 0.040 — the WIDAI KSA's 29 words are well-captured by the DDaT element's 109 words, but the DDaT element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance:
NLI: neutral-dominant (95.5%). "You can:
- put in place processes that identify and document risks across the or" and "Skill in conducting and reviewing Data Protection Impact Assessments, identifyin" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.670 > STS 0.600 (gap +0.071). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [assessments, data, risk, risks, skill] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 9.9%. Shared tokens: [assessments, data, risk, risks, skill]. DDaT-only: [across, appropriately, assessment, assets, caused, compliance]. WIDAI-only: [activities, conducting, high, identified, identifying, impact].
